title: Adopt SwiftUI Observation framework (@Observable) in Rick Terminal
source: Apple Developer docs — Observation framework / WWDC 'Discover Observation in SwiftUI'; migrating off ObservableObject
description: The modern SwiftUI Observation framework (@Observable macro) replaces ObservableObject/@Published and only invalidates views that actually read a changed property, instead of re-rendering every observer. For a live-updating terminal that streams roro events into lists, this cuts wasted redraws and dropped frames when the event feed is busy. Migrating also removes @Published boilerplate and enables fine-grained diffing of the event/ticket view models.
proposed_changes: Replace `class EventStore: ObservableObject { @Published var events }` with `@Observable final class EventStore { var events }`. Drop `@ObservedObject`/`@StateObject` wrappers where the type is now @Observable (use plain `let`/`@State`), keep `@Bindable` only where two-way bindings are needed. Set the app's minimum deployment target to macOS 14+ so the macro is available. Verify list rows subscribe only to the fields they read.
reject_reason: No files were actually modified (false success)
